description: IBM is a legacy enterprise technology company centered on hybrid cloud, artificial intelligence, consulting, infrastructure, and software platforms for large organizations.

overview.title: Business Focus
paragraphs: IBM sells enterprise technology products and services across hybrid cloud, AI, consulting, infrastructure, and software. Its modern strategy is anchored around helping large organizations manage complex computing estates rather than pretending those estates can be made simple by buying one more dashboard.
The company’s software and cloud portfolio includes Red Hat OpenShift for containerized hybrid-cloud operations and watsonx for enterprise AI workflows, data, governance, and model tooling.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: IBM is relevant to the Free The World registry because its products sit deep inside corporate and government technology stacks. Decisions around IBM platforms can influence whether organizations adopt open standards, portable workloads, and auditable AI systems, or remain dependent on large vendor-controlled ecosystems.

The Red Hat acquisition gives IBM a strong open-source story, especially around Linux, Kubernetes, and OpenShift. At the same time, enterprise packaging, support contracts, and managed platform layers mean openness should be evaluated product by product rather than accepted as a logo-level property.

overview.title: Products To Track
paragraphs: Red Hat OpenShift should be tracked as a hybrid-cloud application platform built around Kubernetes, containers, and enterprise operations. It is an important case where open-source foundations and commercial control layers coexist in ways that deserve careful mapping.

watsonx should be tracked as IBM’s enterprise AI and data platform family. Its relevance comes from model governance, AI development tooling, data workflows, and the familiar enterprise promise that centralization will somehow make complexity behave.

sourceSuggestions.title: IBM Investor Relations
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for current business strategy, financial reporting, segment framing, and shareholder-facing priorities.

sourceSuggestions.title: IBM Products
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Canonical product directory for identifying IBM’s current software, cloud, AI, infrastructure, and consulting offerings.

sourceSuggestions.title: IBM watsonx
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary product source for IBM’s enterprise AI platform, including model, data, governance, and AI development positioning.

sourceSuggestions.title: Red Hat OpenShift
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Canonical product source for OpenShift capabilities, deployment models, Kubernetes positioning, and enterprise platform claims.

sourceSuggestions.title: IBM Annual Reports
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful for durable company description, revenue segmentation, risk factors, acquisitions, and management’s official strategic narrative.

sourceSuggestions.title: IBM and Red Hat
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Helps explain how IBM positions Red Hat inside its hybrid-cloud strategy and where open-source infrastructure fits the broader portfolio.
